# ASQL

> A deterministic SQL engine written in Go. Every committed transaction
> gets a monotonic LSN, history is replay-safe, and the primary
> application-facing surface is pgwire (PostgreSQL wire protocol). ASQL
> exposes domain isolation, cross-domain transactions, entity versioning
> with versioned foreign keys, and time-travel reads (`AS OF LSN`,
> `FOR HISTORY`). It is not a drop-in PostgreSQL replacement.

## Project essentials

- [Repository](https://github.com/correodabid/asql): source code, issues,
  and releases.
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/AGENTS.md):
  canonical guide for AI agents working in this repo. Determinism
  contract, repo layout, pgwire gotchas, test tiers, commit conventions.
- [README.md](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/README.md):
  human-facing overview with quickstart.

## Documentation (user-facing)

- [Getting Started](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/getting-started/README.md):
  primary onboarding path — install, first database, domains,
  time-travel queries, entities, fixtures, Studio, integration.
- [PostgreSQL Compatibility](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/reference/postgres-compatibility-surface-v1.md):
  the supported pgwire/SQL surface and intentional gaps.
- [Architecture One-Pager](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/architecture/architecture-one-pager-v1.md):
  system design summary.
- [Go Cookbook](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/reference/cookbook-go-sdk.md):
  concrete pgwire / pgx integration recipes.
- [Fixture Format](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/reference/fixture-format-and-lifecycle-v1.md):
  deterministic scenario file contract.

## Operations

- [Production Runbook](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/operations/runbook.md):
  boot, failover, backup, recovery, upgrade.
- [Incident Runbook](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/operations/incident-runbook-v1.md):
  P0/P1/P2 response procedures.
- [SLO Definitions](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/operations/slo-v1.md):
  workload-scoped objectives.
- [Security Disclosure](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/blob/main/docs/operations/security-disclosure-policy-v1.md):
  how to report vulnerabilities.

## Architecture decisions

- [ADR directory](https://github.com/correodabid/asql/tree/main/docs/adr):
  durable rationale for engine-surface choices.

## Ecosystem

- [ASQL Studio](https://github.com/correodabid/asqlstudio): separate
  desktop workspace (Wails + React). Connects via pgwire.

## AI-agent integration

- ASQL ships an MCP server (`cmd/asql-mcp`) that exposes the engine's
  unique primitives — time-travel, row history, domain introspection,
  entity versioning — to any Model Context Protocol client (Claude
  Desktop, Cursor, Zed, ChatGPT Desktop). See the cmd/asql-mcp/README in
  the repo.

## Contract for agents writing code

- Module path: `github.com/correodabid/asql`
- Go toolchain: 1.25+
- Hard determinism rules documented in AGENTS.md — no wall-clock, no
  randomness, no map iteration order dependencies in the engine.
- pgwire protocol: extended query pipeline must stay compatible with
  pgx v5.9+ semantics (notably, `Describe Portal` is skipped for cached
  statements — send `RowDescription` eagerly in streaming Execute paths).
- Tests: `./internal/...` is the default fast loop; `./...` before
  commit; `./test/determinism/...` for engine changes.
- Commit format: `<type>(<scope>): <summary>` with Co-Authored-By
  trailer when an agent contributed.
